name: GLM-4.5-Air (106B-A12B)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 106B total / 12B active, hybrid thinking/non-thinking response modes
note: Compact MoE sibling of GLM-4.5 for efficient agentic deployment; scored 59.8 vs GLM-4.5's 63.2 on Zhipu's 12-benchmark composite at launch.
tpsNote: ~53-55GB minimum at Q4 exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; needs multi-GPU or CPU offload for full 128K context.

results.notes: BFCL V3 (Full), Table 3 of the GLM-4.5 technical report (which covers both GLM-4.5 and GLM-4.5-Air).
Table 5 'Results on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal-Bench'.
MMLU (EM), Table 6 'Results on Commonly Used General Chat Benchmarks'.

Artificial Analysis's own IFBench measurement for GLM-4.5-Air (37.6%), verbatim-confirmed in the JSON payload embedded in BenchLM.ai's model page (AA-data mirror, display-only row). Distinct from the already-tracked self-reported IFEval score (86.3); not currently tracked for this model.

verificationNotes:
Existence and identity confirmed via official Z.ai blog and Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-4.5-Air). paramsB=106, activeParamsB=12 confirmed verbatim on HF card ('106 billion total parameters', '12 billion active parameters'). License MIT confirmed on HF card. Release date 2025-07-28 confirmed (released simultaneously with GLM-4.5 per cometapi article). All six independent benchmark rows (AIME 89.4, MMLU-Pro 81.4, GPQA 75.0, SWE-bench 57.6, LiveCodeBench 70.7, HLE 10.6) verbatim-confirmed on llm-stats.com/models/compare/glm-4.5-vs-glm-4.5-air. Pricing ($0.2/$1.1 per 1M tokens) confirmed on docs.z.ai/guides/overview/pricing. No changes made to the original record; all fields checked out.

2026-07-16: Checked HumanEval and MMMU — both are genuine absences, not unfound data. HumanEval: the GLM-4.5 technical report (arXiv 2508.06471) and Z.ai's own official launch composite (cited in this record's `note` field) use LiveCodeBench/EvalPlus/SciCode/SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench as the coding suite instead of HumanEval; llm-stats.com's live model page (14 tracked benchmarks) and Epoch AI's model page both show no HumanEval entry; sibling records GLM-4.5 (355B) and GLM-4.6 in this corpus likewise carry no HumanEval score, confirming a family-wide move off this saturated legacy benchmark. MMMU: GLM-4.5-Air is confirmed text-only (modalities=["text"], capabilities.vision=false), verified live via llm-stats.com's comparison page ("Multimodal: No" for both GLM-4.5 and GLM-4.5-Air) and Epoch AI ("No multimodal benchmarks evaluated for this model"); MMMU/MMMU-Pro scores exist only for the separately-released vision sibling GLM-4.5V, which is not part of this corpus. No changes made to existing results.

2026-07-18 (round-22 pass, MMMU gap chase): Re-confirmed GLM-4.5-Air is architecturally text-only and MMMU is inapplicable, not merely unpublished. Corpus fields checked first: modalities=["text"], capabilities.vision=false. Independently re-verified via fresh web search (method 1: official launch materials/model family docs; method 2: independent trackers): (a) llm-stats.com's GLM-4.5-vs-GLM-4.5-Air comparison page and its GLM-4.5-Air model page carry no MMMU row and explicitly mark the model non-multimodal; (b) Z.ai's own developer docs (docs.z.ai/guides/vlm/glm-4.5v) and the GLM-4.5V Hugging Face card / arXiv paper (2507.01006, "GLM-4.5V and GLM-4.1V-Thinking: Towards Versatile Multimodal Reasoning") confirm that vision/multimodal capability (and hence any MMMU score) belongs exclusively to the separate GLM-4.5V model, which is built on top of GLM-4.5-Air's text backbone but is architecturally distinct and not the same corpus entry; (c) MarkTechPost and SiliconFlow launch coverage of GLM-4.5V both describe it as a distinct multimodal release layered onto GLM-4.5-Air, reinforcing that the base GLM-4.5-Air checkpoint scored in this record has no vision pathway and therefore cannot produce an MMMU number. No MMMU score exists or can exist for this exact model/config. No changes made to results[]; this supersedes/reconfirms the identical conclusion already logged 2026-07-16.
